own_review.issue: Added import java.util.Optional is never used (leftover from an earlier Optional-based revision of the fix); dead import, may trip checkstyle/IDE warnings.

'-> empty' marker is printed only when the list is null; a non-null empty list prints nothing under the header, so the log output is inconsistent between the two 'no values' states (same at line ~490 for collections).

No regression test added; the Hibernate bot checklist item 'Add test reproducing the bug' is unchecked and the author explicitly asks whether one is needed. Debug-logging-only NPE, so arguably acceptable, but worth flagging.

Fix verified correct: both LoadedValuesCollectorImpl implementations (FollowOnLockingAction.java:330-338 and CollectionLockingAction.java:196-204) lazily initialize entitiesToLock/collectionsToLock and return the raw fields, so getCollectedEntities()/getCollectedCollections() genuinely return null when nothing was registered; the NPE in HHH-20121 is real and the null guards fix it.

points.note: Verified: Optional is never referenced in the file; import is dead.

points.point: Null check for collectedEntities is correct; both LoadedValuesCollectorImpl impls lazily init, consistent with segmentLoadedValues guard
points.note: Accurate, well-verified commentary (segmentLoadedValues at line 495-497 does guard null), but an affirmation rather than an actionable finding.

points.point: Same null guard correctness for collectedCollections, consistent with segmentLoadedCollections
points.note: Accurate affirmation; segmentLoadedCollections at line 506+ guards null.

points.point: Style note: forEach->for-loop is fine; codebase uses explicit null guards rather than Optional.ofNullable().ifPresent()
points.note: Verified: no ifPresent-based null guards in hibernate-core main sources; accurate but non-actionable style commentary that happens to echo the human reviewer's control-flow preference.

exceeds: Caught the unused Optional import, which the human review (made on the earlier revision) never flagged.
Verified the NPE root cause across both LoadedValuesCollectorImpl implementations and cross-referenced the existing null-guard pattern in segmentLoadedValues/segmentLoadedCollections.

points.point: Unused import java.util.Optional — not used anywhere, remove to avoid compiler warnings (line 49)
points.note: Verified real; identical to my own finding.

exceeds: Caught the unused Optional import not present in the human reference.

qualitative: Tiny, clean PR: null guards around lazily-initialized collector lists to fix a debug-logging NPE, where the only real residual defect is the dead java.util.Optional import left over from an earlier Optional-based revision. Both candidates caught exactly that defect, so both land at net +1 with zero fabrications. v4 is the stronger review qualitatively: it verified the NPE root cause in both LoadedValuesCollectorImpl implementations, cross-referenced the existing null-guard pattern in segmentLoadedValues/segmentLoadedCollections (claims I verified line-by-line and found accurate), and addressed the control-flow style question the human reviewer raised — but those extra points are affirmations, not actionable findings, so they earn no rubric credit. v5 is terse but precise and entirely correct. Neither candidate flagged the minor '-> empty' vs empty-list logging inconsistency or the missing regression test, but both are trivial-severity items I did not count as misses.
